input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` never updates after initial load.**
Use `DynamicResource` instead — it re-evaluates when the underlying dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when you swap the merged dictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Activity restart on Android config change.**
Android destroys and recreates the Activity when `uiMode` changes. Suppress this in `Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.UiMode
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `ConfigChanges.UiMode` declared, Android won't restart your activity — instead it calls `OnConfigurationChanged`, where you can detect the new mode and swap your dictionary:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
    var isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for anything theme-related, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead for values that can change at runtime (like theme colors).

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode setting changes** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when configuration changes (like `uiMode` for dark/light mode) occur. In .NET MAUI, you can handle this by adding `ConfigChanges` to the `Activity` attribute to prevent the restart.


Now I'll provide a concise solution addressing both problems.
